task_completion.description: The target image containing text or graphics is loaded into the image editor (GIMP).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP interface is visible with an image (either a photo or a logo) open in the main canvas.

task_completion.description: The Drop Shadow filter dialog is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Drop Shadow' dialog box is open on the screen, typically accessed via the Filters > Light and Shadow menu.

task_completion.description: A visible shadow region is added to the text or graphics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new dark region appears behind or around the main text/graphics elements in the image canvas.

task_completion.description: The drop shadow has a proper offset direction (down and right).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The shadow is shifted downwards and to the right relative to the original text or graphics.

task_completion.description: The drop shadow is appropriately dark.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The shadow is distinctly dark (low brightness/luminance) compared to the background, creating a clear contrast.

task_completion.description: The drop shadow has soft, blurred edges.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The edges of the shadow are not sharp or hard-lined; they show a smooth, blurred transition into the background.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP graphical user interface to apply the effect.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through GIMP menus and interacting with dialog boxes, rather than running terminal commands (like ImageMagick CLI) to process the image.

integrity.description: The drop shadow was actively applied during the session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows the image initially without a shadow, the shadow settings being configured, and the final image with the shadow applied, rather than just opening a pre-edited image.

integrity.description: The agent did not alter the core text or graphics, only applied the shadow.
integrity.visual_evidence: The original text or graphics remain intact and unchanged in content or primary color; only the drop shadow effect is added.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires applying a drop shadow using GIMP 2.10 (Filters > Light and Shadow > Drop Shadow). A successful result must exhibit: 1) a visible shadow region, 2) an offset directed down and right, 3) appropriate darkness (low luminance), and 4) soft/blurred edges. The original image will either be an Unsplash photo or a synthetically generated logo.
